summary: Completed matching for the Kireihana item unit, moving src/melee/it/items/itkireihana.c from NonMatching to Matching. The PR implemented the item state table, spawn logic, motion anim/phys/coll callbacks, item variable and attribute structs, and adjusted sdata2 symbol scoping. The match report credited 17 new matches, +1860 matched code bytes, +88 matched data bytes, and itkireihana .data/.sdata2 reaching 100%. gmresult.c changes appear to be formatting/include-order cleanup rather than the core Kireihana work.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itkireihana.c is built as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Added ItemStateTable it_803F7E28 with five Kireihana motion states wired to itKireihana_UnkMotion0 through itKireihana_UnkMotion4 Anim/Phys/Coll callbacks.

Implemented it_2725_Logic15_Spawned, initializing Kireihana item state from x4_specialAttributes, resetting facing_dir and item command state, setting timers, fall_speed, x70, and calling item setup helpers including it_802D08F0, it_80279CDC, and Item_8026AE84.

Implemented multiple Kireihana transition helpers and callbacks including it_802D0138, it_802D01A0, it_802D03F8, it_802D05D8, it_802D062C, it_802D0774, it_802D07C0, and motion callbacks for states 0-4.
Added baselib/random.h usage for HSD_Randi in Kireihana physics logic, selecting between Item_8026AE84 calls for article IDs 0x2726 and 0x2727.
Added itkireihana_ItemVars and itkireihanaAttributes in itCommonItems.h, and added kireihana to Item.xDD4_itemVar union in it/types.h.
Updated itkireihana.h prototypes, including corrected return type for itKireihana_UnkMotion0_Coll from void to bool and new prototypes for motion callbacks.
Changed config/GALE01/symbols.txt entries at 0x804DD438 and 0x804DD43C from global it_804DD438/it_804DD43C names to local float labels @145 and @177.
Reformatted gmresult.c in several places, expanding compact unrolled loop/goto logic and changing function pointer cast whitespace; no review evidence explains why this unrelated file changed.
smart_moves: Promoted the unit to Matching only after both code and associated data/sdata2 were reported as 100%, avoiding a partial-matching build flag flip.
Modeled per-item runtime state through a named itkireihana_ItemVars member in the Item.xDD4_itemVar union instead of relying only on raw offsets.
Separated item special attributes into itkireihanaAttributes, making spawn and timer setup read from article data rather than hardcoding all behavior.
Preserved item hitlag behavior consistently by assigning entered_hitlag = efLib_PauseAll and exited_hitlag = efLib_ResumeAll across motion transitions.
Used Item_80268E5C mode transitions with existing item flags such as ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE and ITEM_UNK_0x1 | ITEM_HIT_PRESERVE, matching project item-state conventions visible in the diff.
Kept collision callbacks small and transition-oriented, delegating common ground/air collision handling to helpers such as it_8026E248 and it_8027A118.
Converted formerly global-looking sdata2 float symbols back to local @ labels where the matching output required local constants.
decomp_lessons: For item files, 100% matching often requires defining the ItemStateTable in .data as well as implementing the callbacks; code-only recovery may leave data unmatched.
When an item uses xDD4_itemVar storage, add a typed item-specific struct to itCommonItems.h and register it in the Item union so offsets remain readable and reusable.
Article special attributes should be represented as a typed attributes struct when spawn logic pulls timer or physics constants from xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes.
Collision callback return types matter for matching and headers: this PR changed itKireihana_UnkMotion0_Coll to return bool and return the result of it_8027A118.
Local sdata2 constants should not be forced into global it_804D... symbols if the compiler/linker expects local @NNN labels.
The Kireihana motion callbacks show a reusable pattern for falling item states: accumulate x10_fall_speed into a per-item fall_speed, clamp to x14_fall_speed_max, then assign negative y velocity.

Timer-driven item effects can require exact pre/post decrement placement; the diff uses both --timer == 0 and timer-- <= 0 forms in different callbacks, implying matching depended on expression shape.
Unrelated formatting churn, as seen in gmresult.c, is less valuable for postmortem reuse unless tied to a match report or reviewer explanation.
naming_conventions: Item-specific state variable structs follow the lowercase item prefix plus suffix pattern: itkireihana_ItemVars.
Item-specific special attributes follow the lowercase item prefix plus Attributes pattern: itkireihanaAttributes.
Unknown item motion callbacks use the existing itKireihana_UnkMotionN_Anim, _Phys, and _Coll naming pattern until behavior-specific names are known.
Raw-address helper names such as it_802D0138, it_802D01A0, it_802D03F8, it_802D05D8, it_802D062C, it_802D0774, it_802D07C0, and it_802D08F0 were retained where semantic names were not established.
Local float constants in symbols.txt may use @NNN labels rather than global it_804D... names when they are compiler-local data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use small static inline helper functions for repeated transition setup when that helps reproduce call structure; the diff added itKireihana_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline and itKireihana_UnkMotion2_Anim_inline.
Preserve exact decrement semantics on timers: --timer_unk1 == 0, --timer_unk2 == 0, --timer_unk0 <= 0, and timer_unk0-- <= 0 appear in different matched paths.
Use PAD_STACK(8) in Kireihana physics callbacks where stack shape required it.
For data matching, define ItemStateTable entries in the same order as motion state IDs 0 through 4.
For sdata2 matching, adjust symbol visibility/naming from global to local labels when floats are emitted as local constants.
Function pointer and callback signatures in headers should match implementation exactly, especially bool-returning collision callbacks.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice. The only text excerpt is an automated GALE01 match report showing 17 new matches and 100% Kireihana code/data outcomes.
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs that moved an item file from NonMatching to Matching and added an ItemStateTable.
Search for uses of PAD_STACK in item physics callbacks for matching stack layout examples.
Search for PRs that changed sdata2 global symbols to local @ labels for data matching.
Compare Kireihana item vars with neighboring Pokemon item vars such as fushigibana, enteí, raikou, and suikun.
Investigate whether gmresult.c formatting changes in this PR were required by tooling or accidental unrelated churn.
